title: Pharmaceutical Cold Chain Deviation Alerting
app: Cold chain monitoring sensors via MQTT/Edge Hub
spl:
index=healthcare sourcetype="coldchain:sensor"
| eval low=setpoint_c-tolerance_c, high=setpoint_c+tolerance_c
| eval excursion=if(temp_c < low OR temp_c > high, 1, 0)
| where excursion=1
| stats earliest(_time) as start, latest(_time) as end, max(abs(temp_c-setpoint_c)) as max_deviation by location
| eval duration_min=round((end-start)/60,1)
| table location, start, end, duration_min, max_deviation
description: Many pharmaceuticals (vaccines, biologics) require strict temperature control. Excursions can render medications ineffective, creating patient safety and financial risk.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Pharmaceutical Cold Chain Deviation Alerting to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Integrate cold chain sensors across pharmacy, lab, and storage areas. Configure product-specific setpoints and tolerances. Alert pharmacy immediately on excursions for product assessment. **Domain context:** USP <797>/<800> and manufacturer product information define storage; MAP excursions often require QA disposition—Splunk should log excursion duration and product lot if integrated from WMS. **Splunk:** Drive `low`/`high` from a lookup by product SKU rather than a single `setpoint_c` if mixed inventory.
visualization: Time series (temperature vs bounds), Table (active excursions), Duration chart.
knownFalsePositives: Cold chain alerts during short dock handoffs, aircraft delay tarmac holds, or lane mapping errors the logistics partner rebooked the same day.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cold chain monitoring sensors via MQTT/Edge Hub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="coldchain:sensor"` fields `location`, `temp_c`, `setpoint_c`, `tolerance_c`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate cold chain sensors across pharmacy, lab, and storage areas. Configure product-specific setpoints and tolerances. Alert pharmacy immediately on excursions for product assessment. **Domain context:** USP <797>/<800> and manufacturer product information define storage; MAP excursions often require QA disposition—Splunk should log excursion duration and product lot if integrated from WMS. **Splunk:** Drive `low`/`high` from a lookup by product SKU rather than a single `setpoint_c` if mixed…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=healthcare sourcetype="coldchain:sensor"
| eval low=setpoint_c-tolerance_c, high=setpoint_c+tolerance_c
| eval excursion=if(temp_c < low OR temp_c > high, 1, 0)
| where excursion=1
| stats earliest(_time) as start, latest(_time) as end, max(abs(temp_c-setpoint_c)) as max_deviation by location
| eval duration_min=round((end-start)/60,1)
| table location, start, end, duration_min, max_deviation
```

Understanding this SPL

**Pharmaceutical Cold Chain Deviation Alerting** — Many pharmaceuticals (vaccines, biologics) require strict temperature control. Excursions can render medications ineffective, creating patient safety and financial risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="coldchain:sensor"` fields `location`, `temp_c`, `setpoint_c`, `tolerance_c`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cold chain monitoring sensors via MQTT/Edge Hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: healthcare; **sourcetype**: coldchain:sensor. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=healthcare, sourcetype="coldchain:sensor". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **low** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **excursion** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where excursion=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by location** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **duration_min** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Pharmaceutical Cold Chain Deviation Alerting**): table location, start, end, duration_min, max_deviation


Step 3 — Validate
Compare Splunk results with the EHR, LIS, pharmacy, or device vendor view and spot-check fields against the Epic/Cerner (or equivalent) audit export for the same user, patient context, or device and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time series (temperature vs bounds), Table (active excursions), Duration chart.
grandmaExplanation: We watch pharma cold chain in transit and storage. We help you catch breaks before product must be written off.
